user_message: Just came out of account health with Wide World Importers. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-01-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into account health.

[00:05:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:12:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:17:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:26:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:31:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-N trap (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-N failure mode (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-N -- Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
